channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This 18-inch mobile workstation is probably the fastest laptop in the world right now with up to 256GB RAM and an RTX 5090GPU
item.description: 18-inch Schenker laptop packs Intel Core Ultra 9 CPU, RTX GPUs, 256GB RAM, 32TB storage, and advanced cooling options.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: This travel site is the Google Maps helper you never knew you needed
item.description: Trying to get from point A to point B? If only it were that simple! With any manner of travel these days, you’ve got options: planes, trains, buses, ferries, and beyond. And finding the best path to embark on isn’t always easy. Even finding all the available options can sometimes be a pain.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: This music streamer is giving its users a version of Spotify Wrapped every month
item.description: With music streaming, users have gotten used to being at the mercy of algorithms. But French music streamer Deezer is making it easier for its subscribers to make the algorithm work for them.
item.dc:creator: Lana Tleimat, Fast Company

item.title: YouTube Music listeners are getting a very handy new volume control
item.description: A much-requested volume normalization control for YouTube Music appears to be rolling out for some users.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Shrinking season 3: everything we know about what's coming next for the Apple TV+ show
item.description: Shrinking is officially back in production – here’s everything we know about the third season of Apple’s hit therapy series.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Bay Area art school closures continue as Contra Costa School of Performing Arts takes its curtain call
item.description: For nearly a decade, the nonprofit charter school offered a free, project-based learning environment for young artists to explore courses in dance, instrumental and vocal music, theater production, and design.
item.dc:creator: Katie Lauer, The Mercury News

item.title: Fresh iPhone 17 dummy unit leak may show camera bumps and MagSafe connectors for all four models
item.description: We've got another look at the size and the shape of the four models Apple is expected to unveil this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IPVanish's malware protection confirmed among the best on the market
item.description: The technology fueling IPVanish malware and tracker blocker, VIPRE Advanced Security received the highest rating in the latest AV-Comparatives' test.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: What to know about Jared Birchall, Elon Musk’s right-hand man
item.description: A Wall Street Journal report this week gave an extensive look into how Elon Musk, the world’s richest man and a senior Trump adviser, handles his companies and his more than a dozen children. The answer, it seems, is by relying heavily on his longtime right-hand man, Jared Birchall.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix Defies Economic Downturn with Strong Q1 Report
item.description: Netflix reports strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations despite economic uncertainty
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Discord accused of exposing kids to graphic, violent content in lawsuit
item.description: New Jersey Attorney General Matthew Platkin filed a lawsuit against Discord on Thursday, alleging the popular messaging app misled parents about its safety features.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Samsung's latest smartphone has a very simple feature that no other Samsung phone offers right now
item.description: Samsung brings back the removable battery with the rugged Galaxy XCover7 Pro, offering reliability and endurance in challenging work environments.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump administration replaces federal COVID-19 response page with 'lab leak' explainer
item.description: The Trump White House on Friday replaced the covid.gov website that provided resources and information on COVID-19 with a webpage promoting the theory that the pandemic was a result of a laboratory leak in Wuhan, China, which a House GOP panel strongly favored in a report released last year.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Everything leaving Hulu in May 2025
item.description: Hulu has announced all the titles leaving the platform in May 2025 – and there's a surprising amount of documentaries in here.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: State-sponsored actors spotted using ClickFix hacking tool developed by criminals
item.description: Iranians, Russians, and North Koreans have been observed trying to trick their targets into running shady commands on their computers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google previews Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model
item.description: Google has introduced an early preview of its Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model. An early version of the model is available through the Gemini API via Google AI Studio and Vertex AI, according to an April 17 Google blog post.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The US has a single rare earths mine. Chinese export limits are energizing a push for more
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — America’s only rare earths mine heard from anxious companies soon after China responded to President Donald Trump’s tariffs this month by limiting exports of those minerals used for military applications and in many high-tech devices.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Roblox, Discord scrutinized by states over child safety issues
item.description: Roblox, which has 85 million people play games on its site daily, has been criticized for not doing enough to protect its young user base.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Learning how to measure genAI’s impact
item.description: Top picks for generative AI readers on InfoWorld AI will require more software developers, not fewer Data show AI assistants make it significantly faster and easier to develop software, spurring companies to tackle more software projects—and (surprise!) hire more developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia watches its Trump overtures come to naught
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Facebook Groups are fueling a black market for Uber and DoorDash accounts, says a new report
item.description: A new watchdog report uncovers Facebook groups quietly fueling a black market for operational Uber and DoorDash accounts, posing serious safety concerns for riders and customers.
item.dc:creator: Arden Anderson, Fast Company

item.title: Best practices for migrating between public clouds
item.description: Historically, cloud migration usually meant moving on-premises workloads to a public cloud, like Amazon Web Services (AWS) or Microsoft Azure.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 7 simple ways to protect your credit cards while traveling
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson shares several easy ways to keep your credit cards safe from digital thieves while you're traveling this summer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kyndryl launches private cloud services for enterprise AI deployments
item.description: Kyndryl has taken the wraps off a suite of private cloud services designed for enterprise customers that want to rapidly deploy AI applications in production environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A restaurant in Havana defies Cuba's tech struggles with digital menus and a robot waitress
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — For Sonia Pérez, stepping into Havana’s Doña Alicia restaurant felt like entering a movie.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Users receive unprovoked Windows 11 offers after Intune code glitch
item.description: Enterprise users are receiving unprovoked offers to upgrade to Windows 11 due to an issue in Microsoft’s device management tools. The issue is with Microsoft’s Intune software, which typically allows system administrators to manage mobile devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Zuckerberg underscores TikTok competition as Meta fights monopoly allegations
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized Wednesday that his social media company faces stiff competition from TikTok, as Meta seeks to fend off accusations from the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) that it has a monopoly over social networking.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Intel sells off majority stake in its FPGA business
item.description: Intel has spun off its programmable solutions group as a standalone FPGA company, selling a majority stake in the company to a private equity firm. Intel is taking a fairly hefty loss on this deal.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Nvidia announces plans to make AI supercomputers in US
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: South Korean full-stack AI startup Upstage eyes expansion in the US and Japan
item.description: South Korean AI startup Upstage is ramping up global expansion in the US and Japan, positioning its full-stack AI platform to serve enterprise clients.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration
item.description: A key focus will be on modernizing federal technology and software to maximize governmental efficiency and productivity. The post The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Intelligence at the edge opens up more risks: how unified SASE can solve it
item.description: In an increasingly mobile and modern workforce, smart technologies such as AI-driven edge solutions and the Internet of Things (IoT) can help enterprises improve productivity and efficiency—whether to address operational roadblocks or respond faster to market demands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
